text:
🔭 Context engineering & harness engineering (Karpathy/Hashimoto)

The AI engineering stack moved through three eras: prompt → context → harness. Karpathy coined "context engineering" (X, Jun 25 2025) — "the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information." Hashimoto coined "harness engineering" (blog, Feb 5 2026) — building the programmatic wrapper that makes the same agent mistake structurally impossible to repeat (Agent = Model + Harness). "Moved" means centre of gravity shifted; all prior techniques remain valid as sub-disciplines of the wider stack.

Key points:

• Context engineering = what the model SEES. Techniques: system prompt, few-shot examples, RAG/retrieval, conversation history, tool outputs, memory files, compaction. Four strategies (LangChain): Write / Select / Compress / Isolate. Anthropic's Sep 2025 guide reports +29% perf from context editing alone, +39% with memory tools.

• Harness engineering = what the model OPERATES WITHIN. Components: agent loop (ReAct, state persistence), task decomposition (Plan.md artifacts), tool design (schemas, error signaling), permissions/authorization, MCP/skills, evals & CI integration, observability, human-in-the-loop gating. Hashimoto's AGENTS.md is the canonical artifact.

• The divide: context engineering is one component inside the harness. Harness engineers design the system; context engineers optimize what each inference call sees.

• "Three eras" framing is community synthesis — Karpathy and Hashimoto coined their terms independently (8 months apart), not as a joint manifesto.

• Best topology resources: Anthropic "Effective context engineering for AI agents" (Sep 2025) for context; github.com/ai-boost/awesome-harness-engineering for harness; arxiv:2507.13334 for academic context survey.
